QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF Mission Bay · MIT-licensed

CryoClear

The open, real-time particle-picking + junk-triage copilot for cryo-EM.

CryoFSL made few-shot picking accurate. We make picking and junk-cleaning interactive, real-time, and measurable — with a human-in-the-loop that learns live, on a single GPU.

Computer Science · picking + junk ML + UI
Hardware / Systems · GPU, MRC I/O, streaming
Chemistry · scientific story + HITL
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  1/11
The gap

Auto-pickers over-pick junk. Cleaning it is manual, post-hoc, and slow.

  • Topaz, crYOLO, CryoSegNet find particles well — but ship no junk-triage layer for ice, carbon edges, and aggregates.
  • Scientists catch junk by eye, after the run — discard, re-tune, re-run. Hours after collection, not during.
  • Real-time platforms (cryoSPARC Live, Warp) use simple pickers and are heavy / closed.
The opening: nobody ships an open, interactive, real-time junk-triage copilot with a live human-in-the-loop learning loop. That product — not a new picking model — is our contribution.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  2/11

One modular pipeline — swap any picker, triage the junk, learn from the scientist.

proof the picks are real

Green = keep, red = junk, overlaid live. Every accept/reject updates the classifier in-session — and a live scoreboard shows exactly how clean the stack is.

CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  3/11
Live picking · M1
EMPIAR-10017 β-galactosidase · each green ring is one picked particle, red is flagged junk. Pan/zoom is O(1) on an off-screen canvas.

It picks particles — and tells you which ones to trust.

  • Locally-normalised band-pass detector finds candidates across the whole field.
  • Each candidate gets 23 intensity-normalised features (radial profile, edge coherence, sharpness).
  • The classifier paints keep vs junk in real time.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  4/11
Measured, not asserted

Junk triage beats raw picks where it counts: a cleaner stack.

+0.09
kept-set purity vs raw picks
improves on every micrograph (in-sample)
27%
pure junk removed
recall unchanged — no real particles lost
0.36 → 0.49
precision at the clean operating point
~0.6–0.75
junk-rejection F1 (held-out)
Honest ceiling: on β-gal, picking-F1 after triage ≈ raw — the leftover false positives are background that genuinely resembles particles, which no classifier (cryoSPARC's included) can separate. The model's real, measurable value is purity and a clean kept set, not a picking-F1 lift.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  5/11
Honest evaluation

Scored against expert CryoPPP ground truth.

  • Picks matched to CryoPPP's hand-labelled particles within the particle radius.
  • We report precision / recall / F1 per micrograph — and held-out, not just in-sample.
  • Published deep-learning pickers reach ~0.73 F1 on their own protocols; we show that for context, not a head-to-head — the picker is a swappable default, the triage layer is the contribution.
Candidate picks overlaid against CryoPPP ground-truth particle positions.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  6/11
Human-in-the-loop · M2

It learns from your corrections — live.

  • Cold-start: the model begins junk-blind.
  • Box-brush to keep or dump particles in bulk; the junk-rejection F1 climbs as it learns.
  • Full undo / redo (Ctrl/Cmd+Z); every change flows into the export.

This is the "it learns from me" loop that batch CLI pickers don't have.

Junk-rejection F1 climbing over successive corrections, with one-click exports.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot

QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  8/11
The proof · M4

2D class averages — "these picks are real."

  • Reference-free 2D classification of the kept particles.
  • cryoSPARC-style expectation-maximisation: translation + rotation alignment, phase-correlation, resolution annealing.
  • Recognisable, centred protein views emerge from the cleaned stack.
Honest: β-gal is small, low-contrast, few-view — averages won't match cryoSPARC's textbook proteasome crispness without thousands of particles + CTF. The pipeline is industry-shaped; the protein is the limit.
Class-average montage of kept β-gal particles.
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot
QBI Hackathon 2026 · UCSF · MIT  |  9/11
Why it scores

The novelty is the product — and we're honest about what it isn't.

WE BUILT

  • An open, real-time junk-triage copilot with a live HITL learning loop.
  • Swappable pickers + classifiers, 7+ datasets, upload-your-own, clean export.
  • Honest measurement against CryoPPP — held-out, in-sample labelled as such.

WE DO NOT CLAIM

  • A novel picking model or a novel ML algorithm.
  • To beat deep-learning pickers' picking-F1 — swap one in; it's in the menu.
  • cryoSPARC-grade 2D on a hard protein from a laptop.
Rubric fit: Impact (cleaner stacks, live) · Execution (a usable product, not a notebook) · Background variety (CS + hardware + chemistry) · Novelty (the interactive real-time junk-triage UX nobody ships).
CryoClear · open real-time cryo-EM junk-triage copilot

Open source · MIT · single GPU

Picking + junk-cleaning,
interactive, real-time, and measurable.

CryoClear turns post-hoc, manual junk removal into a live copilot that cleans the stack as data streams in — and proves it with honest metrics and 2D class averages.

Runs on one RTX-class GPU
MIT-licensed, fully open
